channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Why was Jack Doherty placed under house arrest? Controversial streamer sparks backlash with ankle monitor video
item.description: Jack Doherty claimed he was placed under house arrest after posting a video showing an ankle monitor and saying he must stay home for the next few months. The update sparked major backlash online, with critics mocking the controversial streamer over his past behavior and legal troubles. Doherty was previously arrested in Florida on drug-related charges and later faced a PGA Tour ban after allegedly trespassing and disrupting an event.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: What happened between Adin Ross and Anthony Fantano after Drake mentioned the streamer in Iceman lyrics?
item.description: Anthony Fantano criticized Drake’s Adin Ross lyric during his Iceman livestream review, leading Adin Ross to respond with harsh comments and personal attacks. The argument quickly spread online because of Fantano’s long history of negative Drake reviews and Ross’ close relationship with the rapper through streaming collaborations and public appearances.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: QTCinderella lands CAA representation amid growing mainstream success
item.description: Twitch streamer and YouTube personality QTCinderella, whose real name is Blaire, has signed with the prominent Creative Artists Agency. This significant deal is expected to propel her career further, opening doors to television appearances, movies, and larger brand collaborations beyond her established online success with events like the Streamer Awards.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Indian man says US is no longer a default upgrade: 3 types of people should not come to US in 2026
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Logan Paul reacts in disbelief as streamer Clavicular admits extreme body enhancement habits linked to controversial looksmaxxing culture online
item.description: Logan Paul was left stunned after influencer Clavicular described extreme body enhancement methods during an Impaulsive podcast episode. The interview quickly went viral because of the influencer’s unusual admissions and controversial opinions. Medical experts featured on 60 Minutes have already warned people that dangerous looksmaxxing practices can lead to serious physical risks and harmful emotional effects over time.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: TikToker Gabbie Gonzalez reportedly planned murder-for-hire attack on Jack Avery
item.description: TikTok personality Gabbie Gonzalez has been arrested in California, accused of plotting a murder-for-hire against ex-partner and singer Jack Avery. Authorities allege the plan, linked to a custody dispute over their daughter, involved an undercover agent posing as a hitman. Her father was also arrested in Florida. The investigation is ongoing.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Indian family of 4 completes 3 months in Texas ICE detention; 12-year-old bleeding in stool, his sister vomiting frequently
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Do not travel for any reason: US Ebola alert for Congo, South Sudan, Uganda; level 3 alert for Rwanda
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Fashion giant 'Mango' founder Isak Andic’s son arrested over tycoon’s death in 2024
item.description: Spanish police arrested Jonathan Andic, son of late Mango founder Isak Andic, in connection with his father's December 2024 death. Initially ruled an accident, the case reopened after inconsistencies in Jonathan's testimony emerged. Family sources express full confidence in his innocence, awaiting further investigation.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Manhole death in NYC: Straight from her Mercedes-Benz, woman falls into uncovered sewer hole
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: What happened to Metronade? Beloved YouTuber Roger Moore reportedly dies suddenly at just 23
item.description: Roger “Metronade” Moore, a 23-year-old content creator known for comedy, gaming, and anime videos, reportedly died in an accident. His family, friends, and Syracuse University remembered him through emotional tributes, praising his kindness, creativity, faith, and impact on millions of followers across social media platforms including Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and Twitch.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: Indian man run over by truck in New Mexico during family trip, parents came to US for planning marriage
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is Harinderpal Athwal? UK Uber Eats driver jailed for over 11 years after hit-and-run killed father
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian student dies in road accident in Australia, was trapped under a car
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: $200 million kitty: Meet Choupette, the world's richest cat, whose lifestyle would make Cleopatra envious
item.description: But even the lower reported number is staggering. If one uses the $1.5 million figure and compares it with the median net worth of an American family, about $192,900 in the most recent Federal Reserve survey, Choupette’s supposed care arrangement works out to roughly 7.8 times that figure. Round it off, and the headline becomes irresistible: this cat may be worth eight times the typical American family. If one uses the higher $4 million rumour, the number rises to about 20.7 times the median American family net worth.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who were Cain Clark and Caleb Vazquez? San Diego mosque shooter's mother called police hours before attack
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The poor English boy crowned King of England at 10, and years later, he worked in the royal kitchen
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 3,500-year-old Mycenaean woman buried with swords finally gets a face and it’s rewriting ancient history
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Steve Jobs’ Career Advice: Why his ‘connect the dots’ quote still guides work; small bets beat big plans
item.description: Steve Jobs' famous advice, 'You can't connect the dots looking forward; you can only connect them looking backwards,' offers a practical guide for navigating career uncertainty. Instead of demanding clarity, finding meaning emerges from experiences. This approach encourages exploration and 'small bets' over meticulous planning, fostering creativity and adaptability in today's dynamic job market.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Iran used Romanian proxies to attack London-based Iranian journalist, UK court hears
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
